title: Neonatal herpes (herpes in a baby) - NHS
text:
elop any blisters on your breast do not feed from it until all the blisters are healed cover up any cold sores, lesions or signs of a herpes infection anywhere on your body to avoid passing on the virus 
Support and advice if you have been affected 
You may find these resources helpful:
cold sores genital herpes herpetic whitlow (whitlow finger) Sands: a charity that provides support for anyone affected by the death of a baby World Health Organization (WHO): herpes simplex virus 
Page last reviewed: 24 July 2025
Next review due: 24 July 2028
